nodes.label: Linear Pipeline Design
nodes.rationale: The pipeline is intentionally linear: each stage has one job and calls the next stage explicitly. No event bus or dependency injection, so the call graph is easy to follow and easy to test.

nodes.label: Flat JSON File Storage
nodes.rationale: Storage is intentionally simple: a flat JSON index plus one file per document is enough at small scale. Beyond a few thousand documents this becomes the bottleneck and should be replaced with SQLite or a proper document store. Flat files are easy to inspect for debugging.

nodes.label: Keyword Overlap Threshold
nodes.rationale: Cross-reference detection is intentionally naive: keyword overlap of three is reasonable for short documents but produces too many false positives on long ones. A real system would use TF-IDF or embedding similarity instead.

nodes.label: Keyword Extraction
nodes.rationale: Current approach strips stopwords and returns unique tokens (simple and fast), but treats all keywords equally and cannot weight by position or distinctiveness.

nodes.label: TF-IDF Weighting
nodes.rationale: Term frequency times inverse document frequency gives higher scores to words distinctive to a document. Worth switching once the index is big enough (50+ documents) for IDF to be meaningful.

nodes.label: Embedding-based Similarity
nodes.rationale: Run each document through a sentence transformer, store the vector, do nearest-neighbor search at query time. Much better recall but adds a dependency and makes the index opaque; keyword approach is at least debuggable.

nodes.label: SQLite Migration
nodes.rationale: SQLite becomes worth it when you need range queries or to update individual fields without rewriting the whole record. The current save_processed rewrites the entire JSON file on every update, which is wasteful.

nodes.label: Repository Pattern
nodes.rationale: Parser, validator, and processor all import from storage directly. A repository pattern would centralize access but adds indirection. Probably not worth it until the storage backend needs to change.

nodes.label: Transactional Write Risk
nodes.rationale: parse_and_save calls validate_document and save_parsed in sequence. If save_parsed fails after validate_document succeeds you get a partially written record. Not a problem at small scale, becomes a problem under load.
